featureName: Sprint 5 — Integration Tests on iOS Simulator with Firebase Emulator
description: Build end-to-end integration tests that run the real app on an iOS simulator connected to Firebase Emulators. Each user story tests a complete user flow: tapping buttons, filling forms, navigating screens, and verifying real Firestore data. Tests use the flutter integration_test framework with Firebase Auth/Firestore/Storage emulators so no production data is touched.

userStories.title: Integration Test Infrastructure Setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the integration test framework configured with Firebase Emulators so all subsequent tests can run against local emulated services.
acceptanceCriteria: Create integration_test/ directory at project root
Add integration_test dependency to pubspec.yaml dev_dependencies if not present

Create integration_test/test_helpers.dart with: initializeTestApp() that calls Firebase.initializeApp() then connects Auth emulator (localhost:9099), Firestore emulator (localhost:8080), Storage emulator (localhost:9199)
Create integration_test/firebase_emulator_config.dart with emulator host/port constants matching firebase.json (auth:9099, firestore:8080, storage:9199, functions:5001)

Create integration_test/test_data.dart with factory methods for creating test users (testPosterUser, testProviderUser, testBothUser) and test tasks (testCleaningTask, testTutoringTask) with realistic Singapore data
Create integration_test/app_test.dart as the main test entry point that imports all test groups
Add a helper function cleanupEmulatorData() that deletes test documents from Firestore emulator after each test group
Add a helper function signInTestUser() that uses Firebase Auth emulator to create and sign in an anonymous test user
Verify the setup compiles: flutter build ios --simulator --debug (or flutter test integration_test/ --no-execute if available)
Update ios/Runner/Info.plist to allow localhost connections if needed (NSAllowsLocalNetworking)
flutter analyze shows 0 errors

userStories.title: Integration Test — Dev Login & Onboarding Flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an integration test that verifies the complete dev login flow: splash → welcome → dev login → role selection → profile setup → home screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Create integration_test/auth_flow_test.dart
Test launches app and sees SplashScreen (logo + 'NeighbourGo' text)
After splash timeout, WelcomeScreen appears with 'Get Started' and 'Dev Login' buttons
Tap 'Dev Login (Simulator)' button
App navigates to RoleSelectionScreen — verify 3 role cards visible (poster, provider, both)
Tap 'Both' role card, verify it becomes selected (check icon or border highlight)
Tap 'Continue' button
App navigates to ProfileSetupScreen — verify Display Name field exists
Enter 'Test User' in Display Name field
Enter 'Integration tester' in Headline field
Select 'Ang Mo Kio' from Neighbourhood dropdown
Tap 'Complete Setup' button
App navigates to HomeScreen — verify bottom navigation bar with 5 items is visible
Verify Home tab is active and shows greeting text with 'Test User'
Test completes without crashes or exceptions
All tests pass with `flutter test integration_test/auth_flow_test.dart -d <simulator_id>`

userStories.title: Integration Test — Post Task End-to-End
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an integration test that verifies the complete 5-step task posting flow and confirms the task is saved to Firestore.
acceptanceCriteria: Create integration_test/post_task_test.dart
Pre-condition: user is logged in and on HomeScreen (use signInTestUser helper + create user doc with role=poster)
Tap the Post Task FAB button in bottom nav
Step 1 (Category): verify 10 category cards visible, tap 'Cleaning' category, tap Next
Step 2 (Description): enter title 'Deep clean 3-room HDB', enter description (min 20 chars) 'Need thorough cleaning for my 3-room HDB flat including kitchen and bathrooms', tap Next
Step 3 (Location): enter 'Ang Mo Kio Ave 3' in location field, select 'Ang Mo Kio' neighbourhood, tap Next
Step 4 (Budget): enter '50' in min budget field, tap Next
Step 5 (Review): verify all entered data displayed (title, category, location, budget), verify urgency defaults to 'Flexible', tap 'Post Task' button
Verify success feedback (snackbar or navigation to task detail)
Verify task document exists in Firestore emulator with correct fields: title='Deep clean 3-room HDB', categoryId='cleaning', budgetMin=50, status='open', posterId matches test user
Navigate back to Home and verify the new task appears in 'Active Tasks' list
All tests pass without crashes

userStories.title: Integration Test — Task Discovery & Filtering
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an integration test that verifies the Discover tab shows tasks correctly and category filtering works.
acceptanceCriteria: Create integration_test/task_discovery_test.dart
Pre-condition: seed Firestore emulator with 5 test tasks across 3 categories (2 cleaning, 2 tutoring, 1 pet_care), all status=open
User is logged in and on HomeScreen
Tap 'Discover' tab in bottom nav
Verify TaskListScreen shows 'Discover Tasks' in AppBar
Verify all 5 tasks are visible in the list (scroll if needed)
Verify each task card shows: title, category badge, budget, time info
Tap 'Cleaning' category filter chip
Verify only 2 cleaning tasks are shown
Tap 'Tutoring' category filter chip
Verify only 2 tutoring tasks are shown
Tap 'All' chip to clear filter
Verify all 5 tasks are shown again
Tap on first task card
Verify TaskDetailScreen opens with correct task title and description
Tap back button to return to Discover
All tests pass without crashes

userStories.title: Integration Test — Bidding Flow (Provider Submits, Poster Accepts)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an integration test that verifies a provider can submit a bid on a task, and the poster can accept it.
acceptanceCriteria: Create integration_test/bidding_flow_test.dart
Pre-condition: seed Firestore with 1 open task posted by 'posterUser', create a second user 'providerUser' with role=provider
Sign in as providerUser
Navigate to Discover tab, find the seeded task, tap to open TaskDetailScreen
Verify 'Submit Bid' button is visible (provider view)
Tap 'Submit Bid' button — verify SubmitBidSheet modal opens
Enter bid amount '65' in the amount field
Enter message 'I have 5 years experience in cleaning' in proposal field
Tap submit button in the sheet
Verify sheet closes and success feedback shown
Verify bid appears in Firestore emulator: amount=65, status='pending', providerId=providerUser.uid
Verify task bidCount incremented to 1 in Firestore
Sign out providerUser, sign in as posterUser
Navigate to task detail of the same task
Verify BidListSection shows 1 bid with amount S$65 and provider name
Tap 'Accept' button on the bid
Verify bid status changes to 'accepted' in Firestore
Verify task status changes to 'assigned' and assignedProviderId = providerUser.uid in Firestore
All tests pass without crashes

userStories.title: Integration Test — Chat Messaging Flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an integration test that verifies two users can exchange messages through the chat system after a bid is accepted.
acceptanceCriteria: Create integration_test/chat_flow_test.dart
Pre-condition: seed Firestore with a task (status=assigned), an accepted bid, posterUser and providerUser, and a chat document linking them
Sign in as posterUser
Navigate to Messages tab in bottom nav
Verify ChatListScreen shows the chat with the task title
Tap on the chat to open ChatThreadScreen
Verify chat screen shows task title in AppBar
Verify empty state or existing messages are displayed
Type 'Hello, when can you start?' in the message input field
Tap send button
Verify message appears in the chat as a sent message (right-aligned bubble)
Verify message document exists in Firestore emulator under chats/{chatId}/messages/
Verify chat document lastMessage updated to 'Hello, when can you start?'
Navigate back to ChatListScreen
Verify last message preview shows 'Hello, when can you start?'
All tests pass without crashes

userStories.title: Integration Test — Profile View & Edit Flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an integration test that verifies users can view and edit their profile, change role, and see updates reflected.
acceptanceCriteria: Create integration_test/profile_flow_test.dart
Pre-condition: user logged in with displayName='Test User', role=both, headline='Tester'
Tap Profile tab in bottom nav
Verify ProfileScreen shows 'Test User' as display name
Verify current role shows 'Both' or equivalent label
Tap 'Edit Profile' menu item
Verify EditProfileScreen loads with pre-filled data: Display Name = 'Test User'
Clear headline field and enter 'Senior Tester'
Enter 'I am an experienced integration tester' in Bio field
Tap Save button
Verify success feedback (snackbar)
Verify navigation back to ProfileScreen
Verify Firestore user doc updated: headline='Senior Tester', bio='I am an experienced integration tester'
Tap 'Change Role' button on ProfileScreen
Verify role picker bottom sheet opens with 3 role options
Tap 'Provider' role
Verify role updates in Firestore user doc to 'provider'
Verify Home tab now shows ProviderHomeScreen (not poster or both view)
All tests pass without crashes

userStories.title: Integration Test — Bottom Navigation & Screen Transitions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an integration test that verifies all 5 bottom nav tabs work correctly and navigation between screens is smooth.
acceptanceCriteria: Create integration_test/navigation_test.dart
Pre-condition: user logged in with role=both
Verify bottom navigation bar has 5 items: Home, Discover, Post(FAB), Messages, Profile
Tap Home tab — verify HomeScreen renders with TabBar ('Find Help' / 'Find Work')
Tap 'Find Work' tab — verify ProviderHomeScreen content loads
Tap 'Find Help' tab — verify PosterHomeScreen content loads
Tap Discover tab — verify TaskListScreen renders with 'Discover Tasks' title
Tap Messages tab — verify ChatListScreen renders with 'Messages' title
Tap Profile tab — verify ProfileScreen renders with user info
Tap Post Task FAB — verify PostTaskScreen opens (full screen, outside shell)
Tap close/back on PostTaskScreen — verify returns to previous tab
Navigate to a TaskDetailScreen via Discover → tap task card
Tap back — verify returns to Discover list
Navigate to EditProfileScreen via Profile → Edit Profile
Tap back — verify returns to ProfileScreen
Verify no stack overflow or duplicate routes during rapid tab switching (tap each tab 3 times quickly)
All tests pass without crashes

userStories.title: Integration Test — Full User Journey (Post → Bid → Assign → Complete)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an end-to-end integration test that simulates a complete task lifecycle from posting through completion.
acceptanceCriteria: Create integration_test/full_journey_test.dart
This is the master test that chains the entire user flow in sequence
Step 1 — Poster posts task: sign in as posterUser, post a 'Cleaning' task with title 'Clean my flat', budget S$60, location 'Bedok', verify task created in Firestore with status=open
Step 2 — Provider bids: sign out posterUser, sign in as providerUser, navigate to Discover, find 'Clean my flat' task, submit bid of S$70 with message, verify bid in Firestore

Step 3 — Poster accepts bid: sign out providerUser, sign in as posterUser, open task detail, verify bid visible, tap Accept on bid, verify task status=assigned in Firestore, verify bid status=accepted
Step 4 — Chat exchange: posterUser sends message 'When can you come?', verify message in Firestore, verify chat lastMessage updated
Step 5 — Task completion: posterUser taps 'Mark as Complete' on task detail, confirm in dialog, verify task status=completed in Firestore, verify completedAt timestamp set
Step 6 — Final state verification: verify task is no longer in 'Active Tasks' on poster home (filtered out), verify all Firestore documents consistent (task, bid, chat)
All steps pass without crashes or exceptions
Test cleans up all created data from Firestore emulator after completion

userStories.title: All Integration Tests Pass & Code Clean
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all integration tests to compile, the codebase to be clean, and a run script for CI.
acceptanceCriteria: flutter analyze shows 0 errors
All integration test files compile without errors: flutter build ios --simulator --debug succeeds

Create scripts/run_integration_tests.sh that: (1) starts Firebase emulators in background, (2) waits for emulators ready, (3) boots iOS simulator, (4) runs flutter test integration_test/ -d <simulator>, (5) kills emulators on exit
Make run_integration_tests.sh executable (chmod +x)
Total integration test count >= 9 test groups covering: auth, post task, discovery, bidding, chat, profile, navigation, full journey
Each test file has proper setUp/tearDown for Firebase emulator data cleanup
Add brief README section or comment in scripts/ explaining how to run integration tests
No regressions in unit tests: flutter test (Sprint 4 tests) still passes
